content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 26, 1997

W.H. "Harry" Armstrong
Mayor Pro Tem
City of Clovis
655 West Stuart
Clovis, California 93612

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-97-356

Dear Mr. Armstrong:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be
construed as evaluation of any conduct which may already have taken place. Further, this letter
is based on the facts as they have been presented to us. The Commission does not act as the
finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

May the expenses for travel, lodging, meals, dues, registration fees and other costs related
to your positions as the Chairman of the Fresno County Democratic Central Committee and as a
member of the Executive Board of the California Democratic Party be paid from your Mayor Pro
Tem officeholder account?²

CONCLUSION

Expenditures from officeholder accounts formed pursuant to Section 85313(a) must be
used for expenses related to assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents, or carrying
out the official duties of the elected officer. Expenditures from officeholder accounts may not be
made in connection with any campaign for elective office or ballot measure. To the extent that
the travel and travel related expenses, dues, registration fees, and other costs arising from your

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Your letter specifies that you wish to use your Mayor Pro Tem officeholder account for the purposes set
forth in your letter. This response is based on that understanding.

428 I STREET, SUITE 800 SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. I-97-356
Page 2

activities as the Chairman of the Fresno County Democratic Central Committee and as a member
of the Executive Board of the California Democratic Party are related to assisting, serving, or
communicating with your constituents or carrying out your official duties as the Mayor Pro Tem
of the City of Clovis, you may use officeholder account funds to pay those expenses.

ANALYSIS

Section 85313(a) provides:

"Each elected officer may be permitted to establish one segregated
officeholder expense fund for expenses related to assisting,
serving, or communicating with constituents, or with carrying out
the official duties of the elected officer, provided aggregate
contributions to such a fund do not exceed ten thousand dollars
($10,000) within any calendar year and that the expenditures are
not made in connection with any campaign for elective office or
ballot measure."

Therefore, expenditures from officeholder accounts formed pursuant to this section, must
be used for expenses related to assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents, or
carrying out the official duties of the elected officer.

As you may be aware, Section 85313(a) is a relatively new provision of the Act, spawned
by Proposition 208 which became effective January 1, 1997. The Commission has not yet had
the opportunity to consider the adoption of any regulations that may help interpret this section,
and thus, we are construing this section on a case-by-case basis according to the specific facts of
each particular matter. In this regard, we recently issued the Bauer Advice Letter, No. I-97-040,
which sets forth some general guidelines to assist officeholders in determining what activities
would constitute "assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents" and "carrying out the
official duties of the elected officer." I have included a copy of the Bauer Advice Letter for your
review.

With respect to the specific facts of your matter, it does not appear that, pursuant to the
Bauer guidelines, your involvement in the Fresno County Democratic Central Committee or the
California Democratic Party would constitute "carrying out the official duties of an elected
officer." Your participation in these groups may qualify as "assisting, serving, or
communicating with constituents" if you can establish that your activities (and, consequently, the
expenses related to them) are related to that purpose (and are not made in connection with any
campaign for elective office or ballot measure). You have not provided us with the details of
your activities as Chairman of the County Democratic Central Committee or as a member of the

³ We do acknowledge, however, that as Mayor Pro Tem of the City of Clovis, you are an elected officer
under Section 82020.

File No. I-97-356
Page 3

Executive Board of the California Democratic Party, so we cannot determine if those activities will qualify as "assisting ... or communicating with constituents." Therefore, we cannot provide you with a definitive answer as whether you may use officeholder account funds to pay or reimburse expenses resulting from these activities.4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:LLD:tls

_________________________

4 Because of the lack of detail in your letter, we are providing you with informal assistance only as required by Regulation 18329. Informal assistance provides no immunity to the requestor. (See Regulation 18329(c).)

sections.analysis:
Section 85313(a) provides:
“Each elected officer may be permitted to establish one segregated
officeholder expense fund for expenses related to assisting,
serving, or communicating with constituents, or with carrying out
the official duties ofthe elected officer, provided aggregate
contributions to such a fund do not exceed ten thousand dollars
($10,000) within any calendar year and that the expenditures are
not made in connection with any campaign for elective office or
ballot measure.”
Therefore, expenditures from officeholder accounts formed pursuant to this section, must
be used for expenses related to assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents, or
carrying out the official duties ofthe elected officer.
As you may be aware, Section 85313(a) is a relatively new provision of the Act, spawned
by Proposition 208 which became effective January 1, 1997. The Commission has not yet had
the opportunity to consider the adoption of any regulations that may help interpret this section,
and thus, we are construing this section on a case-by-case basis according to the specific facts of
each particular matter. In this regard, we recently issued the Bauer Advice Letter, No. 1-97-040,
which sets forth some general guidelines to assist officeholders in determining what activities
would constitute “assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents” and “carrying out the

review.
With respect to the specific facts of your matter, it does not appear that, pursuant to the
Bauer guidelines, your involvement in the Fresno County Democratic Central Committee or the
California Democratic Party would constitute “carrying out the official duties of an elected
officer.”

communicating with constituents” if you can establish that your activities (and, consequently, the

Executive Board ofthe California Democratic Party, so we cannot determine ifthose activities
